summary: Iteration 3 covers the product-first pivot: SplashLoader → ProductHero → XeniaOS as first-class hero (no fullscreen 3D scene). Backend 5/5 pytest pass (root, POST/GET /api/access, idempotency, 422 validation) and POST /api/email/generate SSE stream returns real Claude Sonnet 4.6 tokens end-to-end via curl. Frontend: SplashLoader mounts on first paint and fades within 1.2s. Only 1 <canvas> in DOM — cursor-trail. No leftover R3F scene. All hero test-ids present (xn-nav, bh-mark, nav-cta, product-hero, xenia-os, value-strip, request-access, footer, ambient-audio-toggle, cursor-trail). XeniaOS is fully interactive: all 18 sidebar items (dashboard/tasks/pipeline/signals/research/companies/contacts/leads/campaigns/email/chat/analytics/reports/activity/team/integrations/notifications/settings) switch active module and receive .os-nav-active (royal-blue tint) class. ⌘K opens command-palette via both Meta+K and Ctrl+K and via topbar-command chip; Escape closes it. Companies module → clicking a company row opens CompanyDetail with detail-draft-outreach which deep-links into Email Writer (module-email mounts). Email Writer streaming works: clicking email-regenerate switches label to 'Xenia is writing…' with spinning BlackHoleMark and streams real tokens ('Amelia, this stage usually…') into the <pre> body within seconds. RequestAccess with a unique email returns 200 and access-confirmation ('You're on the list.') renders. No React console errors or warnings observed after filtering benign devtools message.
critical_code_review_comments: Experience.jsx correctly gates content behind a 1.2s splash and, on prefers-reduced-motion, drops to 200ms — good.
No fullscreen R3F canvas remains; only CursorTrail's HTML canvas is on the page. Matches the pivot brief.
Nav.jsx: royal-blue CTA uses .bg-royal token + shadow; BlackHoleMark is the logo. Good.
XeniaOS.jsx keyboard handler covers Meta+K, Ctrl+K, and Escape at window scope — consistent with previous iteration.
EmailWriter.jsx: SSE parser handles multi-line events and event: done/error. BlackHoleMark shown as AI indicator while generating. Verified with live stream.
RequestAccess.jsx: POST /api/access is idempotent server-side; friendly toast on error; access-confirmation renders correctly.

Backend server.py: previous iteration's benign hydration warning ('<span> inside <option>') has been mitigated in EmailWriter — labels are precomputed into single-text-node constants before rendering as <option> children. No React warnings surfaced this run.
Minor (non-blocking): server.py still uses deprecated @app.on_event('shutdown'); FastAPI recommends lifespan handler. Non-functional.

Minor (non-blocking): AmbientAudio.jsx references scrollState progress driven by the old cinematic scroll; with the new short page, progress will rarely exceed ~0.3 — audio still plays but the timbre-by-act logic is largely dormant. Consider simplifying or removing act-driven modulation now that acts are gone.
seed_data_creation: One TEST_ prefixed AccessRequest row per test run (harmless lead-capture rows).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Page is now product-first with an ~1.2s SplashLoader gate; always wait ~1.8s after networkidle before asserting hero visibility. Only 1 <canvas> should exist (cursor-trail). XeniaOS sidebar exposes exactly 18 nav-{id} buttons with corresponding module-{id} panes. ⌘K responds to Meta+K, Ctrl+K, and topbar-command; Escape closes. Deep-link flow: nav-companies → any [data-testid^='company-c-'] row → CompanyDetail → detail-draft-outreach → module-email mounts with target company preselected. Email streaming endpoint is real Claude Sonnet 4.6; expect first tokens in <2s.
rca of the issue: N/A — no regressions found. The prior iteration_2 concern about React hydration warning from <span> inside <option> in EmailWriter has been resolved (labels are precomputed to single text nodes).
